steps.reveal: Tempus = the time (nom. sg., subject of 'erat')

hints.accusative: While 'tempus' can be accusative, here it is the nominative subject of 'erat'
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'temporis' — 'tempus' is nominative here
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'tempore' — 'tempus' is nominative, subject of 'erat'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'temporis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'temporis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'temporis' ends in -is

hints.to go: That would be 'ire' — 'erat' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' — 'erat' comes from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to plow: That would be 'arare' — 'erat' comes from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'est' — 'erat' is imperfect indicative of 'esse'
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'fuit' — 'erat' is imperfect indicative
hints.imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'esset' — 'erat' is imperfect indicative

hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.aro, arare, aravi, aratum: These are the parts of 'arare' (to plow)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'quietem' — 'quies' is nominative singular

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'quietis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'quietis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'quietis' ends in -is

hints.ablative: While 'mortalibus' could be ablative, here it is dative of reference — rest 'for' mortals
hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'mortales' — 'mortalibus' is dative/ablative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'mortalium' — 'mortalibus' is dative here

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'mortalis' ends in -is (3rd declension i-stem adj.)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'mortalis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'mortalis' ends in -is

hints.ablative: While 'aegris' could be ablative, it agrees with 'mortalibus' which is dative here
hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'aegri' — 'aegris' is dative plural
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'aegrorum' — 'aegris' is dative, modifying 'mortalibus'

translation: It was the time when first rest comes to weary mortals
